questions.question: Implement the bubble sort algorithm to sort an array of integers. What is the time complexity of this algorithm?
questions.hint: Bubble sort is a simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.

questions.question: Write a program to implement the selection sort algorithm. How does this algorithm differ from bubble sort?
questions.hint: Selection sort is another simple sorting algorithm that works the same way as bubble sort, but it always selects the smallest element from the unsorted portion of the list.

questions.question: Explain the differences between insertion sort and selection sort in terms of worst-case and average-case time complexities. Provide examples to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: Insertion sort has a time complexity of O(n^2) in both worst-case and average-case scenarios, while selection sort has a time complexity of O(n^2) in the worst-case scenario but O(n) in the best-case scenario.
